title: Multi-property scoping for taxes, vouchers, and upsells
summary: Reference for the multi-property + multi-room scoping pattern on taxes, vouchers, and upsells: schema, applies-to-all semantics, the pricing-query shape, partner API impact, and the shared UI helpers.
content:
## What changed

Taxes, vouchers, and upsells can now apply to **multiple** properties and rooms, via new `property_ids INTEGER[]` columns (and existing array/CSV `room_ids`). Previously each record could target one property and one room only, which forced duplication when a tax or voucher genuinely applied to several.

**Surfaces touched:** GAS Admin Apply-To UI (3 modals), partner API (2 endpoints), pricing engine (5 query sites + 1 JS helper).

---

## Schema reference

| Table    | `property_id` (legacy) | `property_ids` (new) | `room_id` (legacy) | `room_ids`            |
|----------|------------------------|----------------------|--------------------|------------------------|
| taxes    | INTEGER                | INTEGER[]            | INTEGER            | INTEGER[]              |
| vouchers | INTEGER                | INTEGER[]            | INTEGER            | INTEGER[]              |
| upsells  | INTEGER                | INTEGER[]            | INTEGER            | TEXT (CSV) — see below |

**CSV asymmetry on upsells.** `upsells.room_ids` stays TEXT (comma-separated string) for legacy reasons. Conversion happens at the API boundary:

- SQL: `string_to_array(room_ids, ',')::int[]`
- JS: `Array.isArray(val) ? val.join(',') : val`

Not migrated to INTEGER[] to avoid a destructive migration. If you're touching upsell save code, look for the `roomIdsCsv` pattern in `/api/admin/upsells` POST/PUT for the boundary conversion.

---

## The applies-to-all semantic

`null`, `undefined`, and `[]` **all** mean "applies to all properties (or rooms)". An empty array is **not** "scoped to nothing".

Example: a sitewide tourist tax saved with no Apply-To selections gets `property_id = NULL` and `property_ids = NULL`. The pricing query's first OR-arm `(property_id IS NULL AND property_ids IS NULL)` matches it for every property.

This matters at every save site — sending `property_ids: []` from the frontend must be normalised to `NULL` at the boundary, not stored as a literal empty array. The save endpoints do this with:

```js
const propertyIdsArr = Array.isArray(property_ids) && property_ids.length > 0 ? property_ids : null;
```

---

## Backwards compatibility — the legacy column write

When a caller sends `property_ids[]` to POST/PUT (admin or partner), the server **also** writes the first element to the legacy `property_id` column. So anything still scanning `property_id` directly without the `OR $1 = ANY(property_ids)` pattern continues to see a sensible value.

```js
const propId = property_id || (propertyIdsArr ? propertyIdsArr[0] : null);
```

**Doesn't matter for:** anything using the new endpoints — they read both columns.

**Matters for:** legacy queries, partner GETs that don't yet read the array, Beds24 sync paths, receipt generation, anything stale that scans the legacy column.

---

## The pricing pattern (SQL)

**INTEGER[] form** (taxes, vouchers, and the property scope on upsells):

```sql
WHERE (
  (property_id IS NULL AND property_ids IS NULL)
  OR property_id = $propId
  OR $propId = ANY(property_ids)
)
AND (
  (room_id IS NULL AND room_ids IS NULL)
  OR room_id = $roomId
  OR $roomId = ANY(room_ids)
)
```

**CSV form** (upsells.room_ids only):

```sql
AND (
  (room_id IS NULL AND (room_ids IS NULL OR room_ids = ''))
  OR room_id = $roomId
  OR $roomId = ANY(string_to_array(room_ids, ',')::int[])
)
```

**Where this pattern lives in server.js:**

- `/api/public/calculate-price` — tax SELECT and voucher scope check
- `calculateLocalQuote` helper — tax SELECT
- `/api/public/upsells/:unitId` — upsell SELECT (uses a CTE for the unit lookup)
- Auto-cleaning suppression lookup
- `voucherMatchesScope(voucher, propertyId, roomId)` — JS mirror of the same rules at module scope, used when the row is already loaded

---

## Voucher scoping is now enforced

**Before Phase 6:** validation only inspected `property_ids[]`. Legacy `property_id` was silently ignored. The Apply-To UI looked like it worked but pricing accepted any voucher on any property.

**After Phase 6:** scope is enforced via `voucherMatchesScope`. Any voucher created with property or room scope set will refuse on a non-matching booking.

At first rollout (2026-04-29) 11 active scoped vouchers were audited; all scopes matched intent. Property owners using cross-property voucher codes (intentionally or by accident of the silent-ignore bug) will need to be told their codes only work where named.

---

## Partner API integrators (Adi at Elevate)

- **`/api/partner/.../taxes`** and **`/api/partner/.../upsells`**: `property_ids` is now an additive optional field on POST/PUT, and present on GET responses. URL `propertyId` still anchors the legacy `property_id` column. Existing Elevate integration code with no changes continues to work.
- **`/api/partner/.../vouchers`**: does **not** exist yet. If/when added, follow the same pattern as taxes/upsells.
- Swagger (`public/api-docs.html`) updated for taxes and upsells. Voucher Swagger does not yet exist.

---

## Shared UI helpers — for any future Apply-To section

All in `public/gas-admin.html`. Six functions in one block:

- `loadPropertyCheckboxes(containerId, inputName, roomContainerId, roomInputName)`
- `loadRoomCheckboxes(containerId, inputName, propertyIds)`
- `handleAllCheckChange(checkbox, inputName, roomContainerId?, roomInputName?)`
- `handleItemCheckChange(checkbox, inputName, roomContainerId?, roomInputName?)`
- `getSelectedCheckboxIds(inputName)` → `int[] | null`
- `restoreCheckboxSelection(inputName, ids)` — no-op when `ids` is null/empty

**Drop-in pattern** for any future Apply-To section (offers v2, fees, etc.):

1. Two sibling divs in the modal HTML: `id="X-properties-list"` and `id="X-rooms-list"`.
2. On modal open: `await loadPropertyCheckboxes(...)` then `await loadRoomCheckboxes(..., null)`.
3. On save: pull `getSelectedCheckboxIds('X-properties')` and `getSelectedCheckboxIds('X-rooms')` into the request body.
4. On edit: load helpers, then `restoreCheckboxSelection('X-properties', existingIds)`.

**Don't re-create per-feature helpers.** During Phases 2–5a we deleted 9 such orphans (`loadVoucherPropertiesForModal`, `loadVoucherUnits`, `handleVoucherAllUnitsChange`, etc.). Don't reintroduce them.

---

## Things deliberately *not* changed

- **`upsells.room_ids` stays TEXT (CSV).** Conversion at the API boundary, not via schema migration. A future migration could clean this up; it isn't load-bearing.
- **The `/api/v1/*` namespace** at `server.js` line ~65085 uses an older schema (`is_active`, `applicable_rooms`) and was not updated. Treat as legacy / out of scope.
- **Upsell line-item lookup by id** in `/api/public/calculate-price` (`SELECT * FROM upsells WHERE id = $1`) is still unscoped — the cart UI presents scope-filtered options upstream. If you ever add a public API that takes `upsell_id` from an untrusted caller, **re-validate scope at that endpoint**.

---

## Commit trail (for archaeology)

13-step rollout shipped as 7 commits:

| Phase | Commit | Scope |
|-------|--------|-------|
| 1     | `aefd305` | DB migration: `property_ids INTEGER[]` on taxes/vouchers/upsells |
| 2 + 3a/b + 4a/b + 5a | `c0ee7c9` | Shared UI helpers; tax/voucher modal HTML + JS; upsell modal HTML |
| 5b    | `52270ef` | Upsell modal JS wiring (openAdd / edit / save / quickAdd) |
| 3c    | `6586d1a` | Tax endpoints (admin + partner) + Swagger |
| 4c    | `2432d5b` | Voucher endpoints (admin only — no partner/Swagger surface exists) |
| 5c    | `fe83c7e` | Upsell endpoints (admin + partner) + Swagger |
| 6     | `945c9fc` | Pricing engine reads multi-property + multi-room scopes |

---

## Debugging — did my [tax / voucher / upsell] apply?

Three SQL snippets to run when a customer reports a code didn't work or a tax was missing. Substitute `$1 = property_id`, `$2 = bookable_unit (room) id`.

**Taxes:**

```sql
SELECT id, name, property_id, property_ids, room_id, room_ids, active
FROM taxes
WHERE active = true
  AND (
    (property_id IS NULL AND property_ids IS NULL)
    OR property_id = $1
    OR $1 = ANY(property_ids)
  )
  AND (
    (room_id IS NULL AND room_ids IS NULL)
    OR room_id = $2
    OR $2 = ANY(room_ids)
  );
```

**Vouchers:**

```sql
SELECT id, code, name, property_id, property_ids, room_id, room_ids, active,
       valid_from, valid_until, max_uses, uses_count
FROM vouchers
WHERE active = true
  AND (
    (property_id IS NULL AND property_ids IS NULL)
    OR property_id = $1
    OR $1 = ANY(property_ids)
  )
  AND (
    (room_id IS NULL AND room_ids IS NULL)
    OR room_id = $2
    OR $2 = ANY(room_ids)
  );
```

If the customer's voucher row appears here for their property/room, scope is fine. Investigate other gates next: `valid_from`/`valid_until`, `max_uses` vs `uses_count`, `min_nights`, `single_use_per_guest`.

**Upsells:**

```sql
SELECT id, name, property_id, property_ids, room_id, room_ids, active
FROM upsells
WHERE active = true
  AND (
    (property_id IS NULL AND property_ids IS NULL)
    OR property_id = $1
    OR $1 = ANY(property_ids)
  )
  AND (
    (room_id IS NULL AND (room_ids IS NULL OR room_ids = ''))
    OR room_id = $2
    OR $2 = ANY(string_to_array(room_ids, ',')::int[])
  );
```
